title: SIX PRINCIPLES OF SURRENDER | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Thank you.
Hare Krishna, a warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session.
The topic for the day is 6 principles of surrender.
In the month of August, we had a session on surrender, but in that particular session,
we covered only 3 principles.
So, although there are 6 principles, we covered 3 principles and in today's session, we will
briefly cover those first 3 principles and talk at length, in detail, the next 3 principles.
So, before we get into the discussions, I request all of you to kindly fold your hands
and we will all offer the prayers together.
Seek the blessings of Shila Prabhupada.
Lord Chaitanya, Lord Shri Krishna and with their mercy, get into this discussion.
In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Amen.
In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
So, what is surrender?
Surrender is also called sharanagati.
Surrender means to give oneself to, yield to someone's power or control.
Just like at times we have seen in wars, people raise their both hands and say,
I surrender to you.
They yield their control or power.
They surrender to someone else.
And in other words, they are saying, now that I am surrendering to you, do not shoot me.
Do not kill me.
I am willing to yield myself to your control or your power.
So here, in this case, let us understand what are the principles involved in surrendering
to the Supreme Lord.
Yielding to the power and control of the Supreme Lord.
In fact, in the 18th chapter, towards the end, Krishna says,
sarvadharman parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇam rajaḥ
Surrender to me.
Why surrender is important?
Why Krishna is asking us to surrender to him?
Because, constitutionally, we are amśa.
But, we should not surrender to anyone.
We should not surrender to anyone.
We should not surrender to anyone.
We are part and parcel of the Lord and part and parcel's duty, just like this hands, legs
are part and parcel of this body, the duty of this hands and legs, the part and parcel
is to be surrendered to the well-being of the whole, to the interest of the whole.
Just like if I have some itch in the back, immediately my hand will go there and help
me relieve the itch.
So likewise, we are amsa of the Lord and our constitutional position is to be completely
surrendered to the Supreme Lord, being an amsa of the Lord.
It is not unreasonable, just like this hand, it is not unreasonable for the hand to not
act independently.
It is not unreasonable.
It is best suited if it is serving the interest of the whole.
So likewise, Krishna is requesting maam ekam sharanam raja and we all are expecting to
be happy, wanting to experience bliss, all that is possible if we are situated in that
situation.
And when we surrender to the Lord.
Basically it is saying, Lord, take charge of my life, do not shoot me.
Shoot me with what?
If we are not surrendered to the Lord, we are shot by arrows of maya.
In fact we are killed by arrows of maya.
So if we want to safeguard ourselves, keep ourselves safe from maya, the best thing
for us is to be surrendered to the Supreme Lord.
So Sanatan Goswami, in Hari Bhakti Vilas, talks about six principles of surrender.
As I said, we had covered three of them, surrender, why and how.
This was the session which we had covered in the month of August.
So the first principle is anukulyasya sankalpahav, which means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
This means that surrender to the Lord.
Now we have to remember that in the practice of devotional service we have to accept all
the things which are favorable for Krishna consciousness.
There are many do's and don'ts prescribed by Acharyas, Vaishali Prabhupada.
Some things which we should do, it is good for our spiritual growth, we should accept
wholeheartedly, enthusiastically.
Like for example, we have to worship the Lord.
We should pray to the Lord.
We should worship the Lord.
We should pray to the Lord.
chanting of the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. It is very very important for our
spiritual growth. For example following the Ekadashi Vrata, for example getting
up early in the morning, daily hearing Bhagavatam, Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya, accepting all
those activities, all those instructions which are very favorable for our growth
in Krishna consciousness, if we accept them wholeheartedly, enthusiastically, the
principle is called as Anukulyasya Sankalpah. Anukul means it is conducive, it is
favorable for our spiritual growth. The second principle is Pratikulyasya Varjanam.
So every time we have do's and don'ts, what we should do and what we should not
do. So every time we have do's and don'ts, what we should do and what we should not do.
Even when we go to the doctor, he will give us a medicine, he will also give us
some don'ts. Do not eat oily food, some restrictions with respect to food, take
the medicine after food, do not take the medicine empty stomach. So there are many
many restrictions like four regulative principles, we should not eat meat, we
should not engage in any illicit activities.
We should not do intoxication, we should restrict or restrain ourselves from the activities
which will pull us down, which will degrade our consciousness. So if one is very very
enthusiastic to follow such restrictions, that principle called as Pratikulyasya Varjanam.
Varjanam means to give up. To give up all activities which are unfavorable and heat
unfavorable for Krishna consciousness. Reading newspapers. To be
oppressed with the latest news, it's fine. But reading newspaper for a couple of
hours, watching television shows mindlessly, these all activities, if we
indulge in, it will derail, it will pull our consciousness down. The third
principle is rakshashyati iti vishwaso. To have that vishwas, to have that
conviction, to have that intense feeling that Krishna is my
protector. Rakshashyati iti vishwaso. To have that firm conviction that Krishna
will surely protect me.
In the Mahabharata,
as we all know,
the Pandavas lost the gambling match. It was rigged and they lost even Draupadi
in that match. And we have heard that how Dushashan
attempted to strip Draupadi in front of everyone in the assembly. So when Draupadi
was brought in the middle of that assembly and Dushashan was holding her
sari,
attempting to pull and disrobe her, she pleaded for help, she looked at the five
Pandavas with imploring eyes as if imploring, please step forward and save
me, see what's happening. But none of them came forward. In fact the heads were

down in shame, looking downwards. Although Draupadi implored, pleaded for help, the Pandavas, the Bap más, were not the firsts to ask help, the Pandavas were the last. It was the humble narrowminded people. To have all the good people—now, let us go on.
It is a struggle not to be ashamed but it is a struggle not to be ashamed of things. That constructed
totally walking in the world-tcially.
I keep nothing dirty except for a few of the good ones. It is a struggle
of two people to survive. Because Word has a face that helps you not to give everything away, but I do, without any fear, I love, can I save
implored, pleaded for help but nobody stepped forward.
Then she turned her face to Bhishma, the Grandshire of the Kuru dynasty.
She expected that Bhishma would not allow this injustice to happen, that too in an open
assembly in front of everyone.
She asked for help from Bhishma, even Bhishma did not step forward.
Then finally she decided in a very very helpless state of mind that I will have to fend for
myself.
I will have to protect myself.
So Dushashan was holding the saree and trying to disrobe her and Draupadi with both hands
very tightly held to her saree as if she will not allow Dushashan to pull that saree.
But Dushashan was very powerful.
This is Draupadi.
Relatively a powerful person.
A weak lady, relatively speaking as far as physical strength is concerned.
There was no match between the power of Dushashan and Draupadi.
And Draupadi tried holding the saree with both hands but she soon realized that she
will not be able to save herself.
So she raised one hand and said Krishna Govinda and still one hand is holding on to the saree.
But again she felt that she cannot save herself.
So she raised one hand and said Krishna Govinda and still one hand is holding on to the saree.
But again she felt that she is helpless.
And finally realizing that she is utterly dependent on the mercy of Govinda.
She cannot protect herself.
She has to be dependent on the Lord.
She completely surrendered to Govinda, raised both hands and said Govinda.
And when she finally surrendered to the Lord with both hands raised, having given up all
attempts to protect herself.
With her might, with her strength, finally Krishna supplied unlimited strength of, unlimited
length of saree.
On one side Dushashan was trying to pull and he could never disrobe Draupadi.
In fact Krishna sometimes takes devotees, his good devotees, sincere devotees to a situation
where they have no option but to surrender to the Lord.
In fact Kunti Maharani, when the Pandavas got the kingdom back, she is requesting Krishna,
Krishna now that we have got the kingdom back, we will all be safe, we all will be living
amidst comforts, luxuries, opulence, we will forget you.
She is praying to Krishna, oh Krishna give us more and more miseries.
Because when we were in forest, we were in the forest.
Different demons were attacking me, we were completely dependent on you, on your mercy,
we were surrendered to you.
Now that we will be comfortably situated in our kingdoms, we will forget you.
So Kunti Maharani is praying, oh Lord give us more and more miseries.
Prahlad Maharaj was completely surrendered to the Lord.
Hrndikashyapu was very unhappy that Prahlad, his son, was completely surrendered to the
Lord.
He was spending time, devoting time talking about Krishna, remembering Krishna, chanting
Krishna's holy name.
So he felt very hurt, in fact he scolded the teachers who were put in charge of schooling
or teaching Prahlad Maharaj.
And then finally knowing that Prahlad Maharaj is not willing to stop, he is continuing to
do his devotional activities.
He finally tried punishing Prahlad.
He threw him from the top of a cliff.
He threw Prahlad in a pit full of snakes.
He tried crushing Prahlad under the feet of some elephants.
But Prahlad was completely surrendered to the Lord, not at all fearful, completely dependent
on the Lord.
And confident, abhashya siddhiti rakshasate, Lord will surely protect me.
Just like a child in the lap of mother, especially a young child, is completely dependent on
the mother.
Mother, if the mother forgets to feed the child, the child will cry, but will not make
a sound.
The child will cry.
The child will cry.
The child will cry.
The child will cry.
The child will cry.
She cannot make its own arrangement.
In fact, it cannot make its own arrangement.
So in that sense, completely surrendered.
So the mother is always conscious of the child.
Same way, when we are completely surrendered to the lord, completely dependent on the lord,
dependant on the protection of the lord, the lord also takes charge of such a devotee.
And the surrender is reciprocal.
Krishna in the Gita says,
As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate.
If our surrender is 100%, we are completely dependent on the Lord,
then Krishna's reciprocation is also 100%.
If our surrender is 0%, we are completely dependent on our own protection,
our money, our bank balance, our relatives, our own power, our own possessions,
then all the best.
Krishna has given us free will.
We can use that free will to surrender to the Lord or not surrender to Him.
And surrender, by the way, is not black or white.
Either we are surrendered or not surrendered.
Just like between black and white, there are different grades of shape
or...
of shades of grey colour.
Likewise, there are different degrees of surrender and spiritual advancement means
we surrender to the Lord more and more.
Yesterday, I received a question from one devotee who is practicing Krishna consciousness very sincerely.
She asked me,
is it okay to get vaccinated?
So I said yes.
I asked her, is it something wrong in the sense from the perspective of is there some non-vegetarian...
In fact, there was some news that while manufacturing vaccine, they use some ingredients which is taken out from bovine
calves, something to do with non-vegetarian ingredient.
So, the question was that vaccine is material and we are devotees,
why should we depend on something material for our protection?
Why not depend on Krishna?
It's a very valid question.
If we are talking about surrender,
then why don't we completely be surrendered to the Lord and make no attempt to protect ourselves.
We don't have to wear a mask or do some hand sanitization or do some precautions.
We just be dependent on the Lord.
Actually, the two things which our scriptures talk about Anu Karan and Anu Sharan.
Anu Karan means we imitate the activities of pure devotees or completely surrender devotees and Anu Sharan means we follow in their footsteps.
Anu Karan would be doing Rasa dance because Krishna also did Rasa dance.
I imitate the activities of the Lord.
He danced with the Gopis.
So I can also do that.
That's called Anu Karan, which is not recommended.
Anu Sharan means following in the footsteps of great Acharyas of the Lord and following their instructions.
We cannot imitate.
Lord Shiva drank whole ocean of water.
Lord Shiva drank whole ocean of poison and kept it in his neck.
That's why it's called Neelkanth.
As devotees of Lord Shiva, we cannot also drink Bhang and all kinds of intoxicant thinking that Lord Shiva also did.
We can also do it.
Krishna danced with Gopis.
So we can also do it.
No.
So there are very advanced devotees who are completely surrendered to the Lord, completely dependent on the Lord.
Who have complete faith.
They have complete faith in the protection of the Lord.
Nishtha, firm faith.
For them, they don't require any protection.
Just like Prahlad Maharaj.
They are very confident that Krishna will protect them.
And because they are fully surrendered,
Krishna also protects such devotees.
But because we are on the path of surrender, we are not fully surrendered.
We cannot imitate such devotees.
We have to follow in the footsteps, which means that as a matter of duty, we go to the doctor.
But maintain that we don't put firm and complete faith.
We don't completely surrender to the medicine, completely surrender to the doctor thinking that he will save my life.
Prahlad Maharaj says we may have the best of doctors, the best of medicines, but if Krishna does not protect,
all this will be of no use.
So we go to the doctor, we take the medicine as a matter of duty.
But knowing that, that medicine will be effective and I will get cured if Krishna sanctions.
If Krishna protects me.
We don't, sometimes people when they get some surgery done, they fall at the feet of the doctor and they say you have given me the second life.
Yes, we can be grateful to the doctor.
But remember, the doctor is a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord.
If I was supposed to get cured, the doctor is a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord.
The ultimate cause, sarvakarana karanam daivan, it is the Supreme Lord.
So that consciousness a devotee has, as a matter of duty, he will make arrangements.
Just like in our temple, there was a couple of years back when the terrorists attacked Akshardham.
So we beefed up the security.
There were a lot of security guards protecting the temple in Bangalore.
Now we cannot say that we should be dependent on Krishna, surrender to Krishna.
Why do we have to depend on security?
Yes, we have to do that as a matter of duty and maintain the consciousness.
We are making arrangements.
But faith is not necessarily faith.
If we have 100% faith on the arrangement, the faith is on the Lord.
By the intelligence given by the Lord, as a matter of duty, we are making the required arrangements.
So that is practical application of faith in the Lord.
Because we are not fully surrendered, we are not pure devotees.
Our surrender has not completely manifested.
Or as I mentioned earlier, a fully surrendered devotee like Prahlad,
need not make such arrangements.
So this is the third principle.
The fourth principle is,
which means that one should have the faith and the conviction that Lord is my maintainer.
He is maintaining me.
Sometimes, you know, a head of a family can get a feeling that
I am maintaining my family.
The children, wife, everyone is dependent on me.
I am the breadwinner for the family.
Again, as a matter of duty, as an immediate cause,
I may be the breadwinner, but ultimately we should have faith
that not only me, my entire family, everyone is being maintained by the Lord.
Just like Brahma is also part of the Creator.
But he is secondary Creator.
The primary, supreme Creator, the cause of all causes.
Govindam Adi Purusham Tamaham Bhajami.
He is cause of all causes.
So yes, as a matter of duty, I may be maintaining some of my family members,
but we should have conviction that not only my family members, including me,
everyone is being maintained by the Lord.
We take many things for granted.
Just like one day if sun does not rise,
the whole, it's very cloudy, we feel so gloomy.
We have taken it for who has made the arrangement that the sun is rising daily,
giving us heat, light, sunshine, brightens up our day.
Who has made arrangements that the water gets evaporated from water bodies,
the clouds get formed,
and it rains.
So all this arrangement, background arrangements are being made by the Lord.
Many times we are not conscious of it.
We are not aware of it.
We are blinded to such arrangements made by the Lord for maintaining us.
So a surrendered devotee always have a feeling,
the consciousness that the Lord is my maintainer.
There is one Sridhar Swami who is a very erudite scholar in our parampara,
who has written a commentary on Srimad Bhagavatam.
Although he was a grahastha, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was very much in appreciation of his wonderful commentary.
And if anyone tried pointing out some mistakes or lacuna in his commentary,
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu would get very angry.
So it so happened that the Sridhar Swami, he was a grahastha,
he was having a wife,
but deep within he had a desire to completely surrender himself
and devote himself completely in the service of the Lord.
In other words, take sannyas.
He was engaged completely in spiritual activities,
and lost interest for all material activities.
And in fact, one day he told his wife that I need your permission.
I want to give up all these material responsibilities
and I want to completely devote myself to serve the Lord.
So the wife understood.
She was a good chaste wife.
So she told him that I am caring.
She was having a child.
She told him that let me, let the child get born.
And after that, you can go.
I'll give you permission.
She agreed.
Sridhar Swami also agreed for whatever the wife had recommended.
She waited for the childbirth.
Unfortunately, while childbirth there were some complications
and the mother expired.
Now the child was all alone.
Again Sridhar Swami, because the understanding he had with his wife
that after the childbirth he can leave home.
Now the child is there, mother is not there.
The child is completely dependent on the father for his maintenance.
So he could not leave home.
And then somehow he accepted and continued to maintain the child.
But deep within he had that longing.
That eagerness.
That how I can completely, cent percent, devote myself.
My energy, my time, my all the abilities, whatever the Lord.
To broadcast the glories of the Lord.
To serve the Lord.
To chant the names of the Lord more and more.
So one day it so happened that from the ceiling,
a small baby lizard,
fell from the ceiling.
And he was sitting and he was watching from a distance.
And although it fell from the ceiling,
it fell to the ground.
It somehow survived.
Not only it survived,
the baby lizard,
suddenly the baby lizard saw one small fly or insect.
And instinctively,
instinctively she pulled out the tongue and caught hold of that insect and ate it.
So that time,
Swami realized who is maintaining this baby lizard.
Somehow the Lord is arranging food even for this thing.
Why should I not have faith and conviction that my baby also will be protected by the Lord.
Again,
Anukaran Anusharan.
We cannot imitate.
Sridhar Swami was a great exalted devotee of the Lord.
Completely surrendered to the Lord.
With pure desire and intention to serve the Lord wholeheartedly.
This is not that we can give up our responsibilities and say Sridhar Swami did that.
We can also do that.
That would be Anukaran.
So,
when he saw that lizard,
being supplied by food,
and being maintained by the Lord,
he said,
even my child will be taken care by the Lord.
And one day,
he left home,
with the child sleeping back in the home.
With that faith,
with that conviction that Lord will take care,
Lord will maintain this child.
So many,
Prabhupada says,
so many hundreds and thousands of elephants in African birds,
lakhs of them,
who is maintaining them?
They eat tons of food.
So who is maintaining them?
The Lord is maintaining them.
So he left the home.
And after a while,
the child,
as usual,
got up from the sleep,
started crying.
And there was a couple passing by that side.
And they were returning from a temple and that couple
had gone to the temple and offered prayers.
They had not,
they were not having a child.
And they had prayed to the Lord for giving them,
blessing them with a child.
And when they saw the child crying,
just out of curiosity to find out why the child is crying,
they entered the house.
And they saw no one there.
They waited for a while,
a couple of hours,
and they realized that this child is abandoned.
They took charge of the child,
took it home,
and were very,
very grateful to the Lord,
that the Lord has,
that the Lord has listened to their prayers
and has blessed them with the child.
So in that sense,
the Lord can do multiple things with one act.
With that one act,
bless Shri Raswami with
fulfillment of the desire to wholeheartedly serve him.
And also this couple who wants,
longing to have a child.
So that's the fourth principle,
which is
Goktritve Varanam Tatha.
The fifth principle,
before we get into the fifth principle,
fifth and sixth principle,
we'll get into some bit of details.
Let us all enthusiastically participate
and have nice kirtans.
Request all of you to participate by if possible.
You can also join and clap your hands
and enthusiastically participate.
And after the kirtans,
we'll resume the discussion
and talk about the fifth and sixth principle.
Let's have kirtan now.
Great.
Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare

The fourth principle we talked about is
To have that strong understanding, feeling that Krishna is my maintainer.
He is maintaining me.
Not only maintaining me, maintaining everyone.
Including so called my dependents.
To have that firm conviction.
Who is maintaining all the animals in dense forest.
Who is providing them food?
It's Lord.
Lord has made wonderful arrangements to maintain the living beings.
Although we see scarcity in this world, people dying of starvation.
It's actually man-made.
We have very badly meddled with the arrangements made by the Lord.
Because of greed, because of pride, because of lust and we have messed up things.
Otherwise this world means this world is complete in itself and it's coming from the complete whole.
The fifth principle is
Atma Nikshepa
Which means that one should have all his activities and desires completely aligned to the desires of the Lord.
In the Bhagavad Gita and through the teachings of great Acharyas, we understand what are the desires of the Lord.
What he wants us to do.
So if our desires and our activities are completely dependent on the Lord and are completely aligned.
Krishna says surrender to me and we are trying our best to surrender to him.
Krishna says manmana, think of me.
We are trying our best to be conscious of Krishna.
Krishna says serve me.
Engage in my activities.
We are trying our best to dovetail our activities in the service of the Lord.
So that means Atma Nikshepa.
Our activities and desires are completely aligned with the desires of the Lord.
We don't have independent desires.
We don't think ourselves independent.
We feel that we are dependent on the Lord.
Our desires should be the desires of the Lord.
The Lord expects we to act in a particular way.
To think in a particular way.
To feel in a particular way.
And we try our best to mould our lives according to the Lord's desires.
And how do we understand what is the Lord's desire?
What the Lord expects us to do, think, feel?
It is through the Acharyas.
The Acharyas, we cannot approach the Lord directly.
Krishna is the property of his pure devotees.
They understand the mind of Krishna.
So through their teachings, through their instructions, if we follow,
we can be completely aligned and understand what the Lord is expecting us from us.
And we can mould our lives accordingly.
So that is Atma Nikshepa.
And the sixth principle is Karpanya.
Karpanya means to believe.
To feel that I am very insignificant.
To be meek and humble.
I am poorest of poor.
Poorest of poor does not mean literally.
A person may be living amidst opulence in a nice house just like we had Raj Rishis.
But to feel that I am completely dependent on the Lord.
The Lord is maintaining me.
I am nothing.
I am an insignificant speck in this creation.
If we don't have this understanding, we may feel that I am great.
I have amassed this much wealth because of my doing, my hard work, my abilities.
Time can come.
Whereas a devotee who is completely surrendered may accomplish big, big things.
Like Shri Prabhupada went to America with 40 rupees at the old age, 70 years old.
And in short 12 years, worldwide movement with 108 temples, farm communities, gurukuls, ashram, 10,000 disciples.
But there was not a speck of pride.
Completely dependent on the Lord, knowing that I am just an insignificant beggar.
I am just a mere puppet in the hands of the Lord.
So surrender devotees feel like that.
I am nothing.
I am not dependent.
I am just an insignificant speck in this creation.
He does not have faith in his abilities.
He has faith in the Lord's mercy.
Whatever is happening, whatever wonderful things are happening,

Anartha Nigriti, all the misgivings, all the wrong conceptions we have,

all the wrong habits we have,
all the activities which we indulge in, which is actually pulling our consciousness down.
